Act I

The Writer

About Me

A mechanical engineer who chose words over machines.
Turns out, both jobs demand the same thing: find the fault, fix the structure, make it move.

I started as a content writer, building confidence through internships and the kind of early work nobody frames but everybody needs. Sharechat was my first real job. It taught me that writing for India means writing for people who think in a language most agencies don't speak in. Kutumb App gave me scale — millions of users, and the humbling experience of writing for communities I was still learning to understand. Litmus Branding gave me the craft: client briefs, brand voice, the discipline of making every word justify its presence. Cog Culture gave me character: the confidence to push back, the instinct to go further than the brief, and the ability to find the real insight before writing a single line.

Every stop taught me something the one before it couldn't. That's not a career path. That's a creative education.

Currently

Cog Culture, Gurugram  ·  Integrated campaigns, digital, social, print

Previously

Litmus Branding (2024–2026)  ·  Brand voice, performance, video scripts

Before that

Kutumb App (2020–2023)  ·  Social copy at scale for millions of vernacular users

Started at

Sharechat (2020)  ·  Where writing for India stopped being theoretical

Formats

OOH  ·  Film  ·  Social  ·  Email  ·  Print  ·  Brand Voice  ·  Taglines

Act II

The Work

Loud Isn't Always Powerful
Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, MVDESocial & Digital

"Loud Isn't Always Powerful."

Brief

Highlight MVDE's quiet-yet-powerful marine engines, solving the excessive engine noise issue faced by the industry.

Approach

Contrast silence with strength to challenge the "loud equals powerful" perception. Four words. One antithesis. A 100-year industry belief, rewritten.

Delivering Consistent Force, Move After Move
Mitsubishi Heavy Industries, MVDESocial

"Delivering Consistent Force, Move After Move."

Brief

Show MVDE's precision engines powering consistent performance across construction sites in India.

Approach

Blend cultural symbolism with brand precision, positioning MVDE as the silent force behind every milestone achieved.

Forget Repair, Restore & Refix
Mackwell PumpsPrint & Social

"Forget Repair, Restore & Refix. With Mackwell, you won't need them."

Brief

Position Mackwell Pumps as a zero-maintenance, repair-free pumping solution for industrial buyers.

Approach

Name the category's three biggest anxieties. Eliminate them in one sentence. No specs, just a promise delivered with total confidence.

View on Instagram
Would you buy gold without a mark?
LP ScrewMagazine Ad

"Would you buy gold without a mark? Then why a screw?"

Brief

Promote the trust and authenticity of LP's marked screws over unbranded alternatives in the market.

Approach

Gold hallmark analogy to make the mark's importance instantly relatable. The client thought we'd borrowed it from somewhere famous. We hadn't.

View on Instagram
Mark Creates Identity. Even on a Screw.
LP ScrewMagazine

"Mark Creates Identity. Even on a Screw."

Brief

Highlight how a distinct mark builds trust and authenticity for LP Screw.

Approach

Tiger stripes as visual metaphor: marks are not decoration, they are identity. Quiet, literary, and completely unexpected for a B2B fastener brand.

View on Instagram
Sahi Dekhen. Sahi Sune. Sahi Bolen. Sahi Chunen.
LP ScrewSocial  ·  Gandhi Jayanti

"Sahi Dekhen. Sahi Sune. Sahi Bolen. Sahi Chunen."

Brief

Celebrate Gandhi Jayanti in a way that connects naturally to LP Screw without feeling forced or generic.

Approach

Extended Gandhi's three monkeys to four — adding "Sahi Chunen" (Choose Right) as the fourth. The fourth frame is the LP Screw logo. The brand becomes the natural, earned conclusion of a cultural truth.

bar ruhe zamin ast hamin asto
Anand JollizRegional Launch  ·  Social

"Bar ruhe zamin ast hamin asto."

Brief

Announce Jolliz's Srinagar launch by blending local pride with the brand's flavour appeal, without sounding like an outsider entering the market.

Approach

Used a Kashmiri poetic line meaning "this Earth is the paradise we carry" as the emotional core. It worked because it sounded like someone from Srinagar said it. Not a Delhi agency trying to.

18 Saal Ki Jeet Ki Cravings. Finally Hui Puri.
Anand JollizSocial  ·  24-Hr Campaign

"18 Saal Ki Jeet Ki Cravings. Finally Hui Puri."

Brief

Celebrate RCB's first IPL win after 18 years with Jolliz as the victory snack partner.

Approach

Conceived, written and live in under 24 hours of the final whistle. Organic shares outperformed every paid post from the same window.

Tikha ya Mitha, Milega Jolliz Mein!
Anand JollizOOH Billboard

"Tikha ya Mitha, Milega Jolliz Mein!"

Brief

Highlight Jolliz as the go-to brand for both sweet and savory cravings, covering every mood.

Approach

Split the billboard in two — tikha vs mitha — Jolliz owning both sides. One line covers the entire brand proposition. Works in two seconds at highway speed.

Act III

The Pitch

Application Campaign

flipkart

Eight campaigns built for Flipkart's real calendar, from real cultural insights. No templates. No festival wallpaper. Every campaign starts from something true about India and ends at something Flipkart can own.

8 Campaigns Developed

Suryavanshi  ·  Independence Day  ·  Teacher's Day  ·  Dussehra  ·  Diwali  ·  Onam  ·  Onam  ·  Christmas

Cultural Moment

Jazba, Junoon, Jigar

"Jab Jazba, Junoon aur Jigar mil jaate hain, toh bina naksha bhi manzil tak pahunch jaate hain."

Two journeys, one heartbeat. Vaibhav Suryavanshi's road to the IPL century cut to the same beat as a Flipkart rider navigating a route no GPS covers.

#WahanSeBhi #WahanTakBhi

Independence Day

Azaad Hai Tera Cart

"Azaad Hai Tera Cart."

Not about a flag. About the quiet shame of checking a price tag and putting something back. Flipkart removed that shame for a generation. This campaign names that freedom.

#AzaadHaiTeraCart

Teacher's Day

Ek Homework Abhi Bhi Pending Hai

"Ek Homework Abhi Bhi Pending Hai."

Everyone has their teacher's number saved. Never called. Flipkart is the one easy step. Live in-app digital report card mechanic included.

#PendingHomework

Dussehra

Mehngai Ka Ravan Jalega

"Mehngai Ka Ravan Jalega."

10 Ravana heads, each named after a real consumer pain. 10-day countdown. Effigy burns the same night the full sale goes live. On-ground at dahan grounds.

#MehngaiKaRavanJalega

Diwali

Darwaza Khula, Lakshmi Devi Aayi

"Lakshmi Devi wahan jaati hain jahan darwaza khula hota hai."

Opening your cart is the same ritual as opening your home. Reframes Diwali shopping as invitation, not indulgence.

#DarwazaKholo

Christmas

Ho Ho Delivered

"Santa delivers once a year. This week, we're competing."

The film: Santa is overwhelmed, opens Flipkart, outsources. The child suspects. The mother neither confirms nor denies.

#HoHoDelivered

Onam  ·  Two Campaigns

Onam, Completed By You

"Onam Tab Poora Hota Hai, Jab Cart Bhi Poora Ho."

Newspaper prints Onam unfinished. Scan any panel, order, and it completes in AR. Live wall in Kochi blooms as Kerala orders rise.

#OnamCompletedByYou

One Pookalam, Built By Every Delivery

"Har Delivery, Ek Petal. Poora Kerala, Ek Pookalam."

Every Flipkart delivery in Kerala becomes one petal in a state-wide pookalam. Diaspora abroad can plant theirs too.

#OnePookalamBuiltByEveryDelivery
